rad_findings_en:
Lungs and pleura:  
In the left lower lobe, an irregular solid nodule with smooth, sharp margins measures up to 5 mm in diameter (series 5, image 161).  
Micronodules in both lungs measure up to 2–3 mm in diameter.  
In the right upper lobe, small areas of consolidation and mild ground-glass opacity are present.  
In both lungs: Pleuroapical fibrosis; predominantly in the upper lobes, interlobular septal thickening and mild ground-glass opacity with a mosaic attenuation pattern; traction bronchiectasis; mild centrilobular and paraseptal emphysema; bronchial contents in some segmental bronchi; predominantly left-sided calcified nodules, measuring up to 6 mm in diameter (left upper lobe); perifissural nodules along the interlobar pleura measuring up to 2 mm in diameter.

Mediastinum and lung hila: No masses or lymphadenopathy.

Heart: The heart is enlarged in transverse dimension up to 13.7 cm. No pericardial effusion. Coronary calcification, qualitatively moderate. The patient is advised to consult with their primary care physician for consideration of preventive risk modification or quantitative ordinal assessment of coronary artery calcification for each of the four main coronary arteries.  
Aortic valve calcification.

Great vessels: The thoracic aorta is not dilated. Calcified atherosclerotic plaques are present in the wall of the thoracic aorta. The main pulmonary artery is not dilated.

Skeletal structures and chest wall: Degenerative changes of the thoracic spine. Decreased density of vertebral bodies: T11 - 132 HU, T12 - 122 HU.

Upper abdomen: Bilateral Bochdalek hernias. Diverticular outpouchings of the transverse colon wall, without signs of inflammation. In the upper pole of the left kidney, a calculus measures up to 2 mm in diameter.
rad_impression_en:
1. CT findings favor bronchopneumonia in the right upper lobe.

2. Solid nodule in the left lower lobe measuring up to 5 mm in diameter. Per 2017 Fleischner Society guidelines, no further CT follow-up is required.

3. Micronodules in both lungs measuring up to 3 mm. Calcified and perifissural nodules in both lungs.

4. Pleuroapical fibrosis, discoid atelectasis, and traction bronchiectasis in both lungs.

5. Mild pulmonary emphysema.

6. Coronary calcification, qualitatively moderate. The patient is advised to consult with their primary care physician for consideration of preventive risk modification or quantitative ordinal assessment of coronary artery calcification for each of the four main coronary arteries. Aortic valve calcification.

7. Decreased vertebral body density consistent with osteopenia.

8. Diverticulosis of the transverse colon.

9. Urolithiasis: Calculus in the collecting system of the left kidney.

10. Bilateral Bochdalek hernias.

DISCLAIMER: AI lung nodule detection was utilized on this study. This system is designed for high sensitivity, potentially identifying benign findings. Please refer to the radiologist's interpretation to assess the clinical significance of any detected findings.
val_findings_en:
Lungs and pleura: Bilateral pleuroapical scarring. Bilateral diffuse mosaic attenuation patterns representing air trapping, more pronounced in the lower lobes, middle lobe and lingual segments, due to bronchial obstruction. The walls of the bronchi of the lower lobes, middle lobe and lingual segments are severely thickened, subsegmental bronchi are obstructed by contents, in some bronchi by calcified contents. In the upper lobes and to a lesser extent in the lower lobes, multiple centrilobular nodules and centrilobular ground-glass opacities. In the upper lobes, thickening of the interlobular interstitium. Micronodules in both lungs measure up to 2–3 mm in diameter. Discoid atelectasis against the background of bilateral Bochdalek hernias.

Mediastinum and lung hila: No masses or lymphadenopathy.

Heart: The heart is enlarged, cardiothoracic ratio is 0.67. No significant pericardial effusion. Coronary calcification, qualitatively moderate. Calcification of the mitral and aortic valves.

Great vessels: The thoracic aorta is not dilated. Calcified atherosclerotic plaques are present in the wall of the thoracic aorta. The main pulmonary artery is not dilated.

Skeletal structures and chest wall: Degenerative changes of the thoracic spine. 
Decreased bone density in the vertebrae at the T11-T12 level to 122 HU (CT signs of demineralization). L1 retrolisthesis, 4 mm, grade I according to Meyerding classification.

Upper abdomen: Diverticulosis of the transverse colon without signs of inflammation. In the upper pole of the left kidney, a calculus measures up to 2 mm in diameter.
val_impression_en:
1. Diffuse bronchial disease with bronchial contents may represent obliterating bronchiolitis or diffuse panbronchiolitis. Distal airways disease in the form of centrilobular opacities. 

2. Cardiomegaly. Calcification of the mitral and aortic valves.

3. Coronary calcification, qualitatively moderate. The patient is advised to consult with their primary care physician for consideration of preventive risk modification or quantitative ordinal assessment of coronary artery calcification for each of the four main coronary arteries.

4. Diverticulosis of the transverse colon.

5. Nonobstructing calculus of the left kidney.

6. L1 retrolisthesis, 4 mm, grade I according to Meyerding classification.

DISCLAIMER: AI lung nodule detection was utilized on this study. This system is designed for high sensitivity, potentially identifying benign findings. Please refer to the radiologist's interpretation to assess the clinical significance of any detected findings.

rad_findings_en:
In both lungs, pleuroapical fibrosis; areas of mild centrilobular emphysema; in the lower lobes of both lungs, micronodules measuring up to 3 mm in diameter; regression of some previously identified nodules in the lower lobes of both lungs.  
No pleural effusion.

Mediastinum and lung hila: No mass lesions or lymphadenopathy.

Heart: No cardiomegaly. No clinically significant pericardial effusion. No coronary calcification.

Great vessels: The thoracic aorta is not dilated. Calcified atherosclerotic plaques in the wall of the thoracic aorta. The main pulmonary artery is not dilated.

Skeletal structures and chest wall: Degenerative changes of the thoracic spine.

Upper abdomen: In the liver, round hypodense lesions with homogeneous structure and smooth well-defined margins, measuring up to 8 × 6 mm (segment 8), with no interval change compared with March 17, 2025. A round heterogeneous lesion in the upper segment of the left kidney due to a small calcification, measuring 1.1 × 0.7 × 0.9 cm, density 44 HU, with negative interval change compared with March 17, 2025.
rad_impression_en:
1. Heterogeneous lesion in the left kidney with negative interval change compared with March 17, 2025, indeterminate. Contrast-enhanced CT or MRI is recommended.

2. Micronodules in the lower lobes of both lungs. Compared with March 17, 2025, positive interval change with regression of some nodules. No nodules are identified that require follow-up per Fleischner Society recommendations.

3. Mild pulmonary emphysema. Pulmonary emphysema on CT is an independent risk factor for lung cancer. Consider enrollment in a low-dose CT (LDCT) lung cancer screening program if the patient meets eligibility criteria; the current study may be used as a baseline for subsequent follow-up.

4. Hypodense liver lesions, stable compared with March 17, 2025 (likely benign cysts not requiring follow-up).

DISCLAIMER: AI lung nodule detection was utilized on this study. This system is designed for high sensitivity, potentially identifying benign findings. Please refer to the radiologist's interpretation to assess the clinical significance of any detected findings.
val_findings_en:
Lungs and pleura: Bilateral pleuroapical scarring.  In the right lower lobe there is a paravertebral area of osteophyte-induced pulmonary fibrosis. In the lower lobes of both lungs, micronodules measuring up to 3 mm in diameter. Complete regression of previously identified nodules in the lower lobes of both lungs. No pleural effusion.

Mediastinum and lung hila: No mass lesions or lymphadenopathy.

Heart: No cardiomegaly. No clinically significant pericardial effusion. No coronary calcification.

Great vessels: The thoracic aorta is not dilated. Calcified atherosclerotic plaques in the wall of the thoracic aorta. The main pulmonary artery is not dilated.

Skeletal structures and chest wall: Degenerative changes of the thoracic spine.

Upper abdomen: In the liver, multiple round hypodense lesions with homogeneous structure and smooth well-defined margins, measuring up to 8 × 6 mm (segment 8), with no interval change compared with March 17, 2025. A round lesion in the upper segment of the left kidney measures 1.1 × 0.7 × 0.9 cm, density 44 HU, previously 0.6 ×0.6 cm, with negative interval change compared with March 17, 2025.
val_impression_en:
1. Compared with March 17, 2025, complete regression of previously identified nodules in the lower lobes of both lungs. No nodules are identified that require follow-up per Fleischner Society recommendations.

2. Soft tissue density lesion in the left kidney with interval growth compared with March 17, 2025, indeterminate. MRI Without and With IV Contrast or CT Without and With IV Contrast can be recommended according to ACR White Paper recommendations (Herts, et al. J Am Coll Radiol 2018;15:264-273).

3. Hypodense liver lesions, stable compared with March 17, 2025 (likely benign cysts not requiring follow-up).

DISCLAIMER: AI lung nodule detection was utilized on this study. This system is designed for high sensitivity, potentially identifying benign findings. Please refer to the radiologist's interpretation to assess the clinical significance of any detected findings.

rad_findings_en:
Lungs and pleura: Pulmonary nodules on series 5 are as follows:
Right middle lobe:
- Solid nodule along the minor fissure measuring 4 mm on image 137.
Right lower lobe:
- Solid nodule along the major fissure measuring 4 mm on image 159.
Left lower lobe:
- Solid subpleural nodule measuring 5 mm on image 210.
In both lungs, solid micronodules measure up to 3 mm. In the right upper and lower lobes, calcified granulomas measure up to 3 mm. No pleural effusion.

Mediastinum and lung hila: No mass or lymphadenopathy is identified.

Heart: No cardiomegaly. No significant pericardial effusion. Coronary calcification is present in the left anterior descending and left circumflex coronary arteries, qualitatively assessed as mild.

Great vessels: The thoracic aorta is not dilated; no wall calcification is identified. The main pulmonary artery is dilated up to 3.0 cm.

Skeletal structures and chest wall: Moderate degenerative changes of the thoracic spine.

Upper abdomen: In the liver, a lesion with density of -1 HU measures 1.0 × 0.7 cm; margins are fairly well-defined; homogeneous.
rad_impression_en:
1. Solid micronodules and nodules in both lungs; per 2017 Fleischner Society recommendations, in high-risk patients, follow-up chest CT in 12 months is needed; in low-risk patients, no follow-up is required. Calcified granulomas in the right upper and lower lobes are consistent with sequelae of prior infection.

2. Coronary calcification, qualitatively assessed as mild. The patient is advised to consult with their primary care physician for consideration of preventive risk modification or quantitative ordinal assessment of coronary artery calcification for each of the four main coronary arteries.

3. Dilated main pulmonary artery (3.0 cm), which may suggest pulmonary hypertension.

4. Hypodense liver lesions (per Management of Incidental Liver Lesions on CT: A White Paper of the ACR Incidental Findings Committee, 2017; in high-risk patients, follow-up MRI of the abdomen in 3-6 months is needed; in low-risk patients, no follow-up is required).

DISCLAIMER: AI lung nodule detection was utilized on this study. This system is designed for high sensitivity, potentially identifying benign findings. Please refer to the radiologist's interpretation to assess the clinical significance of any detected findings.
val_findings_en:
Lungs and pleura: Pulmonary nodules on series 5 are as follows:
Right middle lobe:
- Solid nodule along the minor fissure measures 4 mm on image 137, perifissural lymph node, requires no follow-up. 
Right lower lobe:
- Solid nodule along the major fissure measures 4 mm on image 159, likely intrapulmonary lymph node, requires no follow-up. 
Left lower lobe:
- Subpleural triangular nodule measures 5 mm on image 210, likely intrapulmonary lymph node, requires no follow-up. 
In both lungs, solid micronodules measure up to 3 mm. In the right upper and lower lobes, calcified granulomas measure up to 3 mm. No pleural effusion. Wall thickening and secretions of the segmental and subsegmental bronchi of lower lobes, to a lesser extent in the upper lobes. A small amount of mucus in the trachea and main bronchi. Tiny tracheal diverticula.

Mediastinum and lung hila: No mass or lymphadenopathy is identified.

Heart: No cardiomegaly. No significant pericardial effusion. Coronary calcification is present in the left anterior descending and left circumflex coronary arteries, qualitatively assessed as mild.

Great vessels: The thoracic aorta is not dilated; no wall calcification is identified. The main pulmonary artery is dilated up to 3.0 cm.

Skeletal structures and chest wall: Moderate degenerative changes of the thoracic spine. Small bone islands of the humeral heads and T9 vertebral body (images 177, 179).

Upper abdomen: In the liver, two lesions with density of -1 HU measure up to 8 mm.
val_impression_en:
1. Solid micronodules and nodules in both lungs (likely intrapulmonary lymph nodes); per 2017 Fleischner Society recommendations, in high-risk patients, follow-up chest CT in 12 months is needed; in low-risk patients, no follow-up is required. Calcified granulomas in the right upper and lower lobes are consistent with sequelae of prior infection.

2. Wall thickening and secretions of the segmental and subsegmental bronchi may represent bronchitis.

3. Coronary calcification, qualitatively assessed as mild. The patient is advised to consult with their primary care physician for consideration of preventive risk modification or quantitative ordinal assessment of coronary artery calcification for each of the four main coronary arteries.

4. Dilated main pulmonary artery (3.0 cm), which may suggest pulmonary hypertension.

5. Hypodense liver lesions measure up to 8 mm, per Management of Incidental Liver Lesions on CT: A White Paper of the ACR Incidental Findings Committee, 2017; in high-risk patients, follow-up MRI of the abdomen in 3-6 months is needed; in low-risk patients, no follow-up is required.

DISCLAIMER: AI lung nodule detection was utilized on this study. This system is designed for high sensitivity, potentially identifying benign findings. Please refer to the radiologist's interpretation to assess the clinical significance of any detected findings.

rad_findings_en:
INDICATION: Shortness of breath for 1 year.

FINDINGS:
Lungs and pleura: The lungs are expanded. No emphysematous changes are identified. No focal areas of alveolar filling or consolidation are identified. No findings of interstitial lung disease, honeycombing, or pulmonary fibrosis. No pleural effusion. A single solid nodule in the left upper lobe in segment 4 measures 3 mm (series 4 image 190). On the right, solid polygonal nodules: along the interlobar pleura measuring less than 3 mm (series 4 image 155), along the pleura measuring 4 mm (series 4 image 232), likely intrapulmonary lymph nodes.

Mediastinum and lung hila: No mediastinal mass. A single enlarged paraaortic lymph node measures up to 1.2 cm in short axis (series 3 image 113). No enlarged lymph nodes in the lung hila, within limitations related to the lack of contrast. Several small (shotty) lymph nodes in both axillary regions, unchanged.

Heart: No cardiomegaly. No significant pericardial effusion. No significant coronary artery calcification is identified. Mitral valve calcifications.

Great vessels: No aortic aneurysm. Pulmonary arteries are normal in caliber. Single calcifications of the aortic arch and the infrarenal aorta.

Skeletal structures and chest wall: Moderate degenerative changes of the thoracic spine. A metallic rod is present in the proximal left humerus. Bilateral breast implants are present.

Upper abdomen: Multiple bilobar liver lesions are identified: hypoattenuating, homogeneous, with density up to 12 HU, measuring from 4 mm to 4.7 cm in diameter, largest in segment 2. In one lesion in segment 3 measuring 3.2 cm in diameter, single mural calcifications are present. The adrenal glands are not enlarged, additional lesions are identified.
rad_impression_en:
1. Single enlarged paraaortic lymph node measuring up to 1.2 cm.

2. Single solid nodule in the left upper lobe. No follow-up is required per the updated Fleischner Society guideline 2017. A few small solid nodules, likely intrapulmonary lymph nodes.

3. Mild aortic calcification.

4. Mitral valve calcification.

5. Multiple liver lesions with benign features, likely cysts. No follow-up is required per Management of Incidental Liver Lesions on CT: A White Paper of the ACR Incidental Findings Committee, 2017.

6. Moderate degenerative changes of the thoracic spine.

7. Postoperative changes in the proximal left humerus.

DISCLAIMER: AI lung nodule detection was utilized on this study. This system is designed for high sensitivity, potentially identifying benign findings. Please refer to the radiologist's interpretation to assess the clinical significance of any detected findings.
val_findings_en:
Lungs and pleura: Bilateral scarring. Single area of paraseptal emphysema on the left (series 4 image 56). In the subpleural lower areas, more on the right, there is thickening of the interlobular interstitium. Osteophyte-induced adjacent fibrosis.
In the lung parenchyma, nodules are identified, in series 4 presented as follows:
In the right middle lobe:
- Solid nodule along the interlobar pleura measures 3 mm (image 155), likely perifissural lymph node, requires no follow-up.
- Triangular subpleural nodule measures 4 mm (image 232).
In the left upper lobe:
- Triangular solid nodule measures 3 mm (image 190), likely intrapulmonary lymph node, requires no follow-up.
In the left lower lobe: 
- Triangular solid nodule measures 4 mm (image 194), likely intrapulmonary lymph node, requires no follow-up.
- Triangular solid nodule measures 4 mm (image 217), likely intrapulmonary lymph node, requires no follow-up.
No pleural effusion. 

Mediastinum and lung hila: No mediastinal mass. A single enlarged paraaortic lymph node measures up to 1.2 cm in short axis (series 3 image 113).

Heart: No cardiomegaly, mild enlargement of the left heart. No significant pericardial effusion. Single calcification is  identified in the left anterior descending coronary artery, qualitatively assessed as mild. Mitral valve calcifications.

Great vessels: No aortic aneurysm. Pulmonary arteries are normal in caliber. Single calcifications of the aortic arch and the infrarenal aorta.

Skeletal structures and chest wall: Moderate degenerative changes of the thoracic spine. metallic hardware is present in the proximal left humerus. Bilateral breast implants are present with hypodense areas in the structure.

Upper abdomen: Multiple liver lesions are identified: hypoattenuating, homogeneous, with density up to 12 HU, measuring from 4 mm to 4.7 cm in diameter, largest in segment 2. In one lesion in segment 3 measuring 3.2 cm in diameter, single mural calcifications are present.
val_impression_en:
1. Mediastinal lymphadenopathy in the form of a single enlarged paraaortic lymph node measuring up to 1.2 cm.

2. Multiple triangular solid nodules in both lungs, likely intrapulmonary lymph nodes. No follow-up is required per the updated Fleischner Society guideline 2017. 

3. Subpleural interstitial changes (thickening of the interlobular interstitium) may be cardiogenic or lymphatic associated.

4. Single calcification is identified in the left anterior descending coronary artery, qualitatively assessed as mild. The patient is advised to consult with their primary care physician for consideration of preventive risk modification or quantitative ordinal assessment of coronary artery calcification for each of the four main coronary arteries.

5. Mitral valve calcification. Mild aortic calcification.

6. Multiple liver lesions with benign features, likely cysts. No follow-up is required per Management of Incidental Liver Lesions on CT: A White Paper of the ACR Incidental Findings Committee, 2017.

DISCLAIMER: AI lung nodule detection was utilized on this study. This system is designed for high sensitivity, potentially identifying benign findings. Please refer to the radiologist's interpretation to assess the clinical significance of any detected findings.

rad_findings_en:
Lungs and pleura: Linear atelectasis in the right middle lobe, lingular segments, and in lung bases on the left. Single calcifications in the lungs up to 3 mm. Pulmonary parenchymal nodules persist and in series 5 are as follows:

In the right lung:
— In the upper lobe:
* Perifissural nodule measuring up to 4 mm, polygonal shape (image 105), unchanged.
* Nodule measuring up to 3 mm (image 52), unchanged.

In the left lung:
— In the upper lobe:
* Peribronchial nodules with a tree-in-bud pattern, measuring up to 4 mm (images 74-82), previously of lower density and smaller size (up to 4 mm).
Peribronchovascular nodule measuring up to 6 mm (image 123), unchanged.
— In the lower lobe:
* Subpleural subsolid nodule measuring up to 6 mm (image 97), previously up to 8 mm.

No pleural effusion.
Trachea and central bronchi are patent.

Mediastinum and lung hila: No masses or lymphadenopathy.

Heart: No cardiomegaly. No clinically significant pericardial effusion. Aortic valve calcification. Moderate coronary calcification. The patient is advised to consult with their primary care physician for consideration of preventive risk modification or quantitative ordinal assessment of coronary artery calcification for each of the four main coronary arteries.

Great vessels: Thoracic aorta is not dilated, with wall calcification. Main pulmonary artery is dilated up to 3.4 cm.

Skeletal structures and chest wall: Moderate degenerative changes of the thoracic spine with osteophyte formation. Decreased bone density in the vertebrae at the L1 level to +130 HU (CT signs of osteopenia).

Upper abdomen: Thickening of the body of the left adrenal gland up to 1.1 cm.

Thyroid gland: Heterogeneous due to nodules in both lobes with indistinct margins measuring up to 1.7 × 1.4 cm.
rad_impression_en:
1. CT appearance of single pulmonary nodules: compared with the prior study, there is decreased size of the subsolid lesion in the left lower lobe, increased density of its structure, and increased nodules characteristic of bronchiolitis in the left upper lobe; the above changes may correspond to postinflammatory changes and residual inflammatory changes, respectively. The remaining single solid pulmonary nodules are unchanged. Per 2017 Fleischner Society recommendations, CT follow-up in 6 months is recommended.

2. Dilated main pulmonary artery (3.4 cm), which may suggest pulmonary hypertension.

3. Moderate coronary calcification. The patient is advised to consult with their primary care physician for consideration of preventive risk modification or quantitative ordinal assessment of coronary artery calcification for each of the four main coronary arteries.

4. Atherosclerosis of the thoracic aorta.

5. Moderate degenerative changes of the thoracic spine with osteophyte formation. Decreased vertebral body density consistent with osteopenia/osteoporosis at the L1 level.

6. Thickening of the left adrenal gland (may correspond to hyperplasia): per Management of Incidental Adrenal Masses: A White Paper of the ACR Incidental Findings Committee 2017, CT follow-up is not required.

7. Nodular changes of the thyroid gland: per Managing Incidental Thyroid Nodules Detected on Imaging: White Paper of the ACR Incidental Thyroid Findings Committee 2014, thyroid ultrasound is recommended.

DISCLAIMER: AI lung nodule detection was utilized on this study. This system is designed for high sensitivity, potentially identifying benign findings. Please refer to the radiologist's interpretation to assess the clinical significance of any detected findings.
val_findings_en:
Lungs and pleura: Linear atelectasis in the right middle lobe, lingular segments, and in lung bases, clinically insignificant. Calcified nodules in both lungs up to 3 mm, unchanged, require no follow-up. Pulmonary nodules in series 5 are as follows:
In the right upper lobe:
- Perifissural node measures up to 4 mm (image 105), unchanged, requires no follow-up.
- Solid nodule measures up to 3 mm (image 52), unchanged.
In the left upper lobe:
- Centrilobular nodules with a tree-in-bud pattern measure up to 4 mm (images 74-82), previously of lower density (ground glass) and smaller size (up to 3 mm).
- Elongated nodule measures up to 6 mm (image 123), unchanged, probably bronchocele.
In the left lower lobe:
- Subpleural solid nodule measures up to 4 mm (image 97), previously up to 5 mm.
No pleural effusion. Trachea and central bronchi are patent.

Mediastinum and lung hila: No masses or lymphadenopathy.

Heart: No cardiomegaly. No clinically significant pericardial effusion. Aortic valve calcification. Moderate coronary calcification. 

Great vessels: Thoracic aorta is not dilated, with moderate wall calcification. Main pulmonary artery is dilated up to 3.4 cm.

Skeletal structures and chest wall: Moderate degenerative changes of the thoracic spine with osteophyte formation. Decreased bone density in the vertebrae at the L1 level to +130 HU (CT signs of demineralization). Degenerative changes in the right shoulder joint with large subchondral cysts of the scapula and humeral head.

Upper abdomen: Unremarkable. 

Thyroid: Thyroid is heterogeneous due to nodules in both lobes measuring up to 1.7 × 1.4 cm.
val_impression_en:
1. Centrilobular nodules in the left upper lobe: compared with the prior study, there is decreased size and increased density of them, correspond to post-inflammatory changes of the bronchiolitis. Elongated nodule in the left upper lobe is unchanged, probably bronchocele. Solid and calcified nodules in both lungs are unchanged. ACR Fleischner Society recommendations (MacMahon, et al. Radiology 2017; 284(1): 228-43) suggest the following: No need for follow-up.

2. Dilated main pulmonary artery (3.4 cm), which may suggest pulmonary hypertension. Consider cardiac echo.

3. Moderate coronary calcification. The patient is advised to consult with their primary care physician for consideration of preventive risk modification or quantitative ordinal assessment of coronary artery calcification for each of the four main coronary arteries. 

4. Aortic valve calcification. 

5. Multiple thyroid nodules measure up to 1.7 × 1.4 cm: per Managing Incidental Thyroid Nodules Detected on Imaging: White Paper of the ACR Incidental Thyroid Findings Committee 2014, thyroid ultrasound is recommended.

DISCLAIMER: AI lung nodule detection was utilized on this study. This system is designed for high sensitivity, potentially identifying benign findings. Please refer to the radiologist's interpretation to assess the clinical significance of any detected findings.
